authoritative_sources.title: Understanding your IRS notice or letter
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS is the tax authority issuing the notices; this official guidance defines notice triage, due-date review, dispute support, balance-due response, notice search, and scam handling for the core workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: notice intake, notice classification, due-date triage, and response-path selection
used_for: required facts for notice response packages
scenario constraints for missing notice details and evidence gaps

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 594, The IRS Collection Process
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS collection-process publication covering billing, collection action, payment options, appeal rights, lien and levy context, and collection workflow states.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: collection status review, payment-plan intake, collection correspondence, and escalation for collection actions
used_for: payment-plan and collection action evidence requirements
refusal of unsupported lien, levy, and collection promises

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 5, Your Appeal Rights and How To Prepare a Protest If You Don't Agree
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS appeals publication for disagreement, protest, and evidence expectations; it defines when response support crosses into appeals and professional-position ownership.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: protest packet preparation, dispute escalation, and appeals-boundary review
used_for: protest or response draft artifact requirements

authoritative_sources.title: Payment plans; installment agreements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS payment-plan page for installment agreement workflow, plan status, setup costs, eligibility, default and revision handling, and online application context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-plan intake, payment-plan status support, and collection next-step packaging
used_for: refusal of approval or term-selection promises

authoritative_sources.title: Offer in Compromise
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS OIC program source; it is authoritative for program context and intake vocabulary while establishing that settlement terms require specialized authorized review.
used_for: handoff to authorized tax representative or advisory owner

authoritative_sources.title: About Form 2848, Power of Attorney and Declaration of Representative
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS authorization guidance for power of attorney and declaration of representative; it controls whether a person may represent or communicate for a taxpayer.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization validation, authority-contact gating, and communication review
used_for: refusal of IRS contact without documented scope

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 947, Practice Before the IRS and Power of Attorney
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS publication explaining practice before the IRS, representation, and power-of-attorney mechanics; it anchors the lane boundary between support and authorized representation.
used_for: routing to enrolled agent, CPA, attorney, or other authorized representative

authoritative_sources.title: Treasury Department Circular No. 230
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Internal Revenue Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal regulations governing practice before the IRS and practitioner duties; Circular 230 defines professional standards, due diligence, competence, written advice, and practitioner boundary risks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: professional-judgment review, written-advice boundary, due-diligence escalation, and signoff control
used_for: refusal of tax advice and written advice without authorized owner review
due-diligence and professional judgment escalation triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Statements on Standards for Tax Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA is the recognized U.S. CPA professional body; SSTS provides professional tax-service standards for tax positions, advice, data reliance, and member responsibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: tax-position rationale review, data reliance, advisory boundary, and quality review
used_for: separating evidence packaging from tax-position decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Taxpayer Bill of Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS taxpayer-rights source used to identify when deadlines, appeals, confidentiality, representation, payment, or due-process-sensitive questions must escalate.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: client-rights screening, deadline-risk review, and appeals or collection escalation
used_for: refusal when client rights determine the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 4557, Safeguarding Taxpayer Data
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official IRS guidance for tax professionals safeguarding taxpayer data; it constrains handling of notices, transcripts, returns, identifiers, workpapers, and client communication records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality review, document handling, de-identification, and authorized-audience checks
used_for: safe handling of notices, transcripts, and correspondence logs

workflow_stages.name: service intake and authorization check
workflow_stages.description: Confirm taxpayer/entity context, jurisdiction, tax period, notice or letter number, response due date, engagement scope, Form 2848 or equivalent authorization status, confidentiality constraints, and systems of record before acting.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: notice and account-record triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify the notice or authority correspondence, locate account transcripts, return copies, payment confirmations, prior correspondence, response instructions, and due-date or collection status.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: evidence and rationale package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble source-linked notice response, payment-plan intake, protest draft, or authority-correspondence packets while separating operational evidence packaging from tax-position rationale, advice, or representation.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: stakeholder communication and review gating
workflow_stages.description: Prepare internal or client-safe status summaries with evidence used, missing prerequisites, deadline posture, confidentiality limits, approval state, and next authority interaction.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: resolution confirmation and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Mark the case resolved only when documented response, submission, payment-plan status, authority confirmation, or handoff evidence supports closure; escalate repeat failures, source conflicts, rights-sensitive deadlines, settlement, legal-risk, advisory, or representation questions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: tax notices, IRS or state letters, notice number references, response instructions, envelope or receipt evidence, and response due-date trackers
IRS or state account transcripts, wage and income transcripts, return transcripts, return copies, amended returns, supporting schedules, and tax workpapers
payment confirmations, EFTPS records, check images, installment agreement records, balance-due computations, penalty and interest calculations, and collection correspondence
protest or response drafts, penalty-abatement support, reasonable-cause fact summaries, appeals notes, OIC or financial intake packets, and reviewer rationale notes

Form 2848 or state power-of-attorney evidence, Form 8821 or information authorization evidence, CAF references, engagement letters, statements of work, approval matrices, and client communication permissions
correspondence logs, authority call notes, submission records, proof of mailing or faxing, document-management records, workflow queue status, QA notes, and case closure records
systems_of_record: IRS e-services or transcript retrieval system
state tax authority portal where applicable
quality review or engagement management system

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate taxpayer context, jurisdiction, tax period, notice details, authorization status, engagement scope, systems of record, confidentiality constraints, and approval rules
prepare evidence-backed notice response packages, payment-plan status notes, protest preparation packets, correspondence summaries, and handoff packets without overstating authority
summarize documented IRS or state workflow status, missing records, deadlines, and next authority interaction when supported by source records
escalate: tax advice, tax-position selection, written tax advice, reasonable-cause rationale, penalty-abatement judgment, appeals strategy, settlement terms, legal interpretation, or professional signoff
practice before the IRS or another tax authority, taxpayer representation, authority calls, negotiations, or submissions requiring authorized representative ownership
client rights, collection due process, appeal deadlines, lien or levy risk, or deadline conflicts where professional judgment determines the safe action
client-facing delivery, authority-facing correspondence, contractual commitment, or case closure when approval, authorization, or evidence is missing
refuse: promise acceptance of an installment agreement, offer in compromise, penalty abatement, appeal, lien release, levy release, deadline extension, or settlement
invent tax-position rationale, missing support, response facts, payment evidence, or authority confirmation
contact a tax authority or disclose taxpayer data without documented authorization and engagement scope
reuse notices, transcripts, returns, taxpayer identifiers, or workpapers outside authorized audience and de-identification rules

domain_failure_modes: a notice response is finalized without return support, transcript evidence, proof of payment, reasonable-cause facts, or reviewer rationale
an operational notice coordinator decides a tax position, abatement rationale, protest strategy, or settlement amount instead of escalating
a payment-plan status note promises IRS acceptance or chooses discretionary terms without authority
a Form 2848 or equivalent authorization gap is ignored and the lane contacts a tax authority or discloses transcript details
notice balance, account transcript, payment confirmation, and correspondence logs conflict but the output asserts final taxpayer liability
an imminent authority deadline causes the lane to send unsupported correspondence rather than escalating deadline and professional-judgment risk
confidential taxpayer notices, EINs, SSNs, transcripts, or workpapers are reused in cross-client materials without authorization or de-identification
repeat notices after prior responses are treated as routine instead of escalated for source-of-truth and owner-resolution review
tenant-specific approval policy silently replaces the public baseline and widens the lane into authority-facing signoff

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
A tax notice response lacks supporting documents or position rationale.
A client asks the lane to negotiate or settle beyond documented authority.
Authority deadlines conflict with unresolved advisory questions.
Request for unsupported workaround or discretionary exception

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: IRS sources establish the federal baseline, but state and local authority procedures, forms, deadlines, appeal channels, and authorization equivalents must be supplied or retrieved at runtime for non-IRS matters.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant engagement scope and authorized representative model
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define representation and professional standards, but whether the tenant may advise, represent, draft, submit, or only coordinate depends on the engagement letter, authorization records, owner map, and approval matrix.

IRS taxpayer-data safeguards establish a conservative baseline, but client contracts, authorization, de-identification policy, and approved communication templates determine whether a specific delivery output is permitted.

Collection, payment-plan, and OIC sources support intake and routing, but affordability judgments, settlement terms, financial approval, and tax-risk recommendations require authorized professional or legal review.

notes: Authoritative tax-resolution records are the tenant-designated notice management, transcript retrieval, workflow, document-management, payment, correspondence, CRM, and quality-review systems constrained by IRS, Treasury Circular 230, AICPA, taxpayer-rights, and tenant policy baselines.

The lane stops before formal tax advice, written tax advice, tax-position selection, legal advice, settlement negotiation, representation before a tax authority, final client communication signoff, contractual commitment, and financial approval.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release because all tenant-specific uncertainties are represented as runtime prerequisites, refusal triggers, or orchestrator return conditions.
